obligations.reason: Visible scope contains a dedicated zero-riscy debug unit and core/controller/register-file modules; debug paths are high-risk only if they reach protected CPU state without an authorization or debug-mode guard.
obligations.subject: external/debug requester
obligations.object: CPU architectural state, register file, PC/control state reachable from debug unit
obligations.expected_guard: debug authentication/authorization and debug-mode entry/enable gating before any debug read/write/override of CPU architectural state
obligations.candidate_path: debug interface -> zeroriscy_debug_unit -> core/control/register access path
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists module presence and case statements but not signal-level behavior; inspect RTL for actual connectivity and guards.

obligations.reason: The scope includes a CSR register module with multiple decode cases and decoder/id-stage paths; protected CSR accesses should be mediated by privilege/access checks at decode and write-enable sites.
obligations.subject: CPU instruction stream/software
obligations.object: control/status registers affecting privilege, interrupts, exception control, debug state, and core configuration
obligations.expected_guard: CSR address decode and write/read enables must enforce required privilege/CSR access class and prevent unauthorized writes to protected machine/debug/control CSRs
obligations.candidate_path: CSR instruction decode -> zeroriscy_cs_registers -> machine/status/exception/debug CSRs
obligations.uncertainty: Privilege signals are not shown in the static fact excerpt; verify whether this core implements privilege/security modes and how CSR access legality is enforced.

obligations.reason: Visible modules include a load/store unit, bus interfaces, and fc_demux address-routing cases; memory/peripheral protection depends on correct range checks and preservation of security attributes across routing.
obligations.subject: CPU data master
obligations.object: SoC memory/peripheral address regions reachable via data bus demux
obligations.expected_guard: address-range/PMP/security-domain mediation must be applied consistently to loads and stores before requests are granted/routed to protected memory or peripheral regions
obligations.candidate_path: load/store unit -> data bus/fc_demux -> SoC/TCDM/APB/peripheral regions
required_evidence_slots: load and store parity of checks
obligations.uncertainty: Need RTL decode constants and routing logic to determine whether any protected ranges exist in this scope.

obligations.reason: fc_subsystem instantiates separate instruction demux and core instruction bus; fetch requests into protected/private regions should not bypass data-side or security checks.
obligations.subject: CPU instruction fetch unit
obligations.object: instruction memory regions and fetch-enable control
obligations.expected_guard: instruction fetch routing to protected/secure/private regions must be mediated by execute/fetch permission or security-mode/address checks
obligations.candidate_path: instruction fetch -> if_stage/core_instr_bus -> fc_demux -> memory regions
obligations.uncertainty: Static facts show fetch/control signals but not address-map semantics; inspect includes and demux logic.

obligations.reason: The interrupt controller exposes security/control-related outputs and receives core_secure_mode_i plus event inputs; any software or event-driven update to core control or secure interrupt signaling should be mediated.
obligations.subject: APB software master or event source
obligations.operation: configure/trigger/override core control and secure interrupt state
obligations.object: fetch_en_o, core_clock_en_o, core_irq_sec_o/core_irq_id_o, interrupt controller configuration/state
obligations.expected_guard: writes or events that alter fetch enable, core clock enable, or secure interrupt classification must be gated by APB access control, privilege/security mode, or trusted event-source authorization
obligations.candidate_path: APB/software interface -> apb_interrupt_cntrl -> fetch_en/core_clock_en/interrupt security outputs
obligations.uncertainty: The provided port list is partial for APB signals; inspect module internals and instantiation connections.

obligations.reason: Visible interfaces include test_mode_i and BIST signals; test logic is not inherently vulnerable, but any route to protected memory/state should be covered by test/lifecycle authorization and disabled in normal operation.
obligations.subject: test/BIST interface
obligations.operation: test enable, BIST pattern application, state readout/result exposure, override
obligations.object: memory/BIST controlled arrays, interrupt/controller state, and any state reachable through test_mode_i or bist_* signals
obligations.expected_guard: test/BIST mode must be restricted by lifecycle/test authorization and must not allow unauthenticated read/write/exposure of memory or protected state during normal operation
obligations.candidate_path: test_mode_i / BIST interface signals -> memories/control interfaces
obligations.uncertainty: Only interface definitions and one test_mode_i port are visible in facts; inspect actual use sites.

obligations.reason: The scope includes an fc_hwpe module and SoC bus fabric; non-CPU masters such as accelerators can bypass CPU privilege checks unless separately mediated.
obligations.subject: HWPE/accelerator master
obligations.operation: accelerator memory/peripheral read/write or control request
obligations.object: shared memory, peripheral bus regions, accelerator control/status paths
obligations.expected_guard: hardware accelerator requests must be mediated by address-range/security-domain checks and must not bypass CPU-side protections or access restricted shared memory/peripheral regions
obligations.candidate_path: HWPE/accelerator interface -> fc_hwpe/fc_subsystem -> shared memory or core control
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer does not expose fc_hwpe ports here; inspect RTL to determine whether it is a requester or only control glue.

obligations.reason: The interrupt controller has explicit secure-mode input and secure interrupt output; permission analysis should verify that security classification cannot be forged or misapplied across module boundaries.
obligations.subject: interrupt/event logic and CPU control state
obligations.operation: change/consume security mode or secure interrupt classification
obligations.object: secure mode state and secure interrupt classification/path
obligations.expected_guard: security-mode state and secure interrupt indications must be generated and consumed consistently, with mode changes mediated and not solely controlled by untrusted interrupt/event inputs
obligations.candidate_path: core_secure_mode_i and core_irq_sec_o propagation across interrupt/controller/core boundary
obligations.uncertainty: Need connection tracing in fc_subsystem/core to identify authoritative source and consumers.
